title: Local transform-based data fusion for high resolution dynamic mri
abstract: A method comprising: fusing: (a) a high-resolution static MRI (Magnetic Resonance Imaging) data of a tissue, with (b) a low-resolution dynamic MRI data of the tissue, by performing a local transform of each of (a) and (b), and making local selections based on coefficients of the transformed (a) and (b), to produce synthesized high-resolution dynamic MRI data of the tissue.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A method comprising: fusing: (a) a high-resolution static MRI (Magnetic Resonance Imaging) data of a tissue, with (b) a low-resolution dynamic MRI data of the tissue, by performing a local transform of each of (a) and (b), and making local selections based on coefficients of the transformed (a) and (b), to produce synthesized high-resolution dynamic MRI data of the tissue. 2. The method according to claim 1, wherein: the static MRI data depicts the tissue without the presence of a contrast agent; and the dynamic MRI data depicts the tissue with the presence of a contrast agent. 3. The method according to any one of claims 1-2, wherein the local transform is a pyramid representation of each of (a) and (b). 4. The method according to claim 3, wherein the pyramid is a Laplacian pyramid, and the coefficients are pixel values. 5. The method according to claim 4, wherein the fusing comprises: resampling slices of the low -resolution dynamic MRI data to match a size of slices of the high-resolution static MRI data; realigning the high-resolution static MRI data to the resampled slices of the low -resolution dynamic MRI data; calculating a Laplacian pyramid for the resampled slices of the low -resolution dynamic MRI data; calculating a Laplacian pyramid for the realigned high-resolution static MRI data; comparing every pair of corresponding pixels in every depth of the two Laplacian pyramids, to select a pixel value; and calculating an inverse Laplacian pyramid transform based on the selected pixel values, to reconstruct the synthesized high-resolution dynamic MRI data of the tissue. 6. The method according to claim 5, wherein: the comparing is to determine which pixel of the pair of pixels has the highest value; and the selected pixel value is the highest value. 7. The method according to any one of claims 5-6, wherein each of the two Laplacian pyramids has a depth of between 3 and 7. 8. A computer program product comprising a non -transitory computer-readable storage medium having program code embodied therewith, the program code executable by at least one hardware processor to: fuse: (a) a high-resolution static MRI (Magnetic Resonance Imaging) data of a tissue, with (b) a low-resolution dynamic MRI data of the tissue, by performing a local transform of each of (a) and (b), and making local selections based on coefficients of the transformed (a) and (b), to produce synthesized high-resolution dynamic MRI data of the tissue. 9. The computer program product according to claim 8, wherein: the static MRI data depicts the tissue without the presence of a contrast agent; and the dynamic MRI data depicts the tissue with the presence of a contrast agent. 10. The computer program product according any one of claims 8-9, wherein the local transform is a pyramid representation of each of (a) and (b). 11. The computer program product according to claim 10, wherein the pyramid is a Laplacian pyramid, and the coefficients are pixel values. 12. The computer program product according to claim 11, wherein the fusing comprises: resampling slices of the low -resolution dynamic MRI data to match a size of slices of the high-resolution static MRI data; realigning the high-resolution static MRI data to the resampled slices of the low -resolution dynamic MRI data; 

 calculating a Laplacian pyramid for the resampled slices of the low -resolution dynamic MRI data; calculating a Laplacian pyramid for the realigned high-resolution static MRI data; comparing every pair of corresponding pixels in every depth of the two Laplacian pyramids, to select a pixel value; and calculating an inverse Laplacian pyramid transform based on the selected pixel values, to reconstruct the synthesized high-resolution dynamic MRI data of the tissue. 13. The computer program product according to claim 12, wherein: the comparing is to determine which pixel of the pair of pixels has the highest value; and the selected pixel value is the highest value. 14. The computer program product according to any one of claims 12-13, wherein each of the two Laplacian pyramids has a depth of between 3 and 7. 15. A system comprising: (i) a non-transitory computer-readable storage medium having program code embodied therewith, the program code executable by at least one hardware processor to: fuse: (a) a high-resolution static MRI (Magnetic Resonance Imaging) data of a tissue, with (b) a low-resolution dynamic MRI data of the tissue, by performing a local transform of each of (a) and (b), and making local selections based on coefficients of the transformed (a) and (b), to produce synthesized high-resolution dynamic MRI data of the tissue. (ii) at least one hardware processor configured to execute the program code. 16. The system according to claim 15, wherein: the static MRI data depicts the tissue without the presence of a contrast agent; and the dynamic MRI data depicts the tissue with the presence of a contrast agent. 17. The system according to any one of claims 15-16, wherein the local transform is a pyramid representation of each of (a) and (b). 18. The system according to claim 17, wherein the pyramid is a Laplacian pyramid, and the coefficients are pixel values. 19. The system according to claim 18, wherein the fusing comprises: resampling slices of the low -resolution dynamic MRI data to match a size of slices of the high-resolution static MRI data; realigning the high-resolution static MRI data to the resampled slices of the low -resolution dynamic MRI data; calculating a Laplacian pyramid for the resampled slices of the low -resolution dynamic MRI data; calculating a Laplacian pyramid for the realigned high-resolution static MRI data; comparing every pair of corresponding pixels in every depth of the two Laplacian pyramids, to select a pixel value; and calculating an inverse Laplacian pyramid transform based on the selected pixel values, to reconstruct the synthesized high-resolution dynamic MRI data of the tissue. 20. The system according to claim 19, wherein: the comparing is to determine which pixel of the pair of pixels has the highest value; and the selected pixel value is the highest value. 21. The system according to any one of claims 19-20, wherein each of the two Laplacian pyramids has a depth of between 3 and 7. 22. The system according to any one of claims 15-21, further comprising: an MRI scanner configured to acquire the high-resolution static MRI data and the low- resolution dynamic MRI data.
